projectName: ZK-Intent Guardian: Drift-Proof Agent Execution Layer
hackathon: Microsoft AI Agents Hackathon | Multi-Agent Safety Track | $50K+
description: ZK-Intent Guardian introduces a middleware layer for autonomous agents that enforces 'Intent Drift' constraints via Zero-Knowledge proofs. Unlike standard permission systems, this primitive verifies that every action taken by an agent is cryptographically bound to its initial signed intent signature. The system uses a Circom circuit to generate a proof that the agent's current state and proposed action fall within the pre-defined parameter space of its original authorization. This proof is submi
angle: First ZK-circuit to mathematically prove an agent's current action remains within its original signed intent bounds without revealing the intent itself.
